meta.section: Endpoint Management — Windows (Phase 4 of 7: Windows Update / WUFB)
meta.source: Microsoft Learn (learn.microsoft.com/intune/device-updates/windows + wufb-reports) — portal paths, ring/feature/quality settings grounded via the Microsoft Learn MCP on 2026-07-03.
generalNotes: The update surface lives at Devices > Windows > Manage updates (Windows 10 and later updates) with tabs: Update rings, Feature updates, Quality updates, Driver updates. The app-update pieces (Office, Edge, Defender AV) and the Secure Boot certificate item are Settings Catalog configuration profiles; WUFB Reports is under Reports.

The two 'WUFB' ring rows are the two halves of one Update ring policy: WUFB Behavior = the User experience tab (active hours, deadlines, restart), WUFB Production Ring = the Update settings tab (deferrals). Configure them in the same ring.
If you use BOTH an update ring and a Feature update policy on a device, set the ring's Feature update deferral = 0 so the two don't double-delay feature upgrades.
Assign to Windows device groups; use separate rings (pilot vs broad) for staged rollout of quality/feature updates.

requiredSettings.label: Policy
requiredSettings.value: Devices > Windows > Update rings > (ring) > User experience settings

requiredSettings.label: Automatic update behavior
requiredSettings.value: Auto install at maintenance time, outside active hours (active hours 8 AM–5 PM)

requiredSettings.label: Use deadline settings
requiredSettings.value: Allow; Deadline for quality updates = 2 days; Grace period = 1 day; Auto reboot before deadline = Yes

requiredSettings.label: Option to pause updates
requiredSettings.value: Disable

steps: Intune admin center > Devices > Windows > Update rings > create/open the production ring > User experience settings.
Set active hours (8 AM–5 PM), auto-install at maintenance time, deadline for quality updates = 2 and grace period = 1, auto-reboot before deadline = Yes, disable user pause.
Assign to the Windows device group.
Note (MEDIUM impact): devices install and reboot within the deadline+grace window; users get restart warnings but can't indefinitely defer.

requiredSettings.label: Policy
requiredSettings.value: Devices > Windows > Update rings > (ring) > Update settings

requiredSettings.label: Quality update deferral period
requiredSettings.value: 0 days (install promptly)

requiredSettings.label: Feature update deferral period
requiredSettings.value: 30 days (or 0 if a Feature update policy governs the version)

requiredSettings.label: Microsoft product updates / drivers
requiredSettings.value: Allow

steps: Same Update ring > Update settings tab.
Set Quality update deferral = 0; Feature update deferral = 30 (set 0 if you also use the Feature update policy below); allow Microsoft product updates and drivers.
Assign to the Windows device group.
Note (MEDIUM impact): quality updates land quickly; feature (major) upgrades are held ~30 days for stability.

requiredSettings.label: Policy type
requiredSettings.value: Devices > Windows > Feature updates > Create profile

requiredSettings.label: Feature update version
requiredSettings.value: The specific Windows feature version to hold devices at (controls/prevents the jump to a newer release)

steps: Intune admin center > Devices > Windows > Feature updates > Create profile.
Choose the target Windows feature version to lock devices to, and the rollout option.
Assign to the Windows device group. Remember to set the update ring's feature-update deferral = 0 so the two don't conflict.
Note: LOW impact — controls WHICH feature release devices run; prevents unplanned major upgrades.

requiredSettings.label: Policy type
requiredSettings.value: Devices > Windows > Quality updates > Create > Windows quality update policy

requiredSettings.label: When available, apply without restarting the device (Hotpatch)
requiredSettings.value: Allow

steps: Intune admin center > Devices > Windows updates > Quality updates > Create > Windows quality update policy.
Set 'When available, apply without restarting the device (Hotpatch)' = Allow.
Assign to the Windows device group.

Note: LOW impact / positive — eligible monthly security updates install without a reboot (needs VBS on and an eligible Win 11 Enterprise build; ineligible devices just get the normal cumulative update). Deferral/active-hours settings still apply.

requiredSettings.label: Policy type
requiredSettings.value: Devices > Configuration > Settings catalog profile

requiredSettings.label: Intent
requiredSettings.value: Opt devices into the Windows Secure Boot certificate (UEFI CA) update servicing so Secure Boot keeps validating boot components after the CA rollover

steps: Intune admin center > Devices > Configuration > Create > Windows 10 and later > Settings catalog.
Add the Secure Boot certificate / UEFI CA update servicing setting (System > Secure Boot area) and enable it per the baseline.
Assign to the Windows device group.
Note: LOW impact / invisible — keeps Secure Boot trust current; part of Microsoft's Secure Boot certificate rollover.

requiredSettings.label: Policy type
requiredSettings.value: Devices > Configuration > Settings catalog profile (Microsoft Office update settings)

requiredSettings.label: Update channel
requiredSettings.value: Monthly Enterprise Channel (predictable monthly cadence)

requiredSettings.label: Enable automatic updates
requiredSettings.value: Enabled; optionally set a target version / deadline

steps: Intune admin center > Devices > Configuration > Create > Windows 10 and later > Settings catalog.
Add the Microsoft Office (Office 365 / Microsoft 365 Apps) update settings: set the update channel to Monthly Enterprise Channel and enable automatic updates.
Assign to the Windows device group.
Note: LOW impact — keeps Microsoft 365 Apps patched on a predictable channel.

requiredSettings.label: Policy type
requiredSettings.value: Devices > Configuration > Settings catalog profile (Microsoft Edge Update settings)

requiredSettings.label: Update channel
requiredSettings.value: Stable

requiredSettings.label: Automatic updates
requiredSettings.value: Enabled (allow Edge to auto-update on the Stable channel)

steps: Intune admin center > Devices > Configuration > Create > Windows 10 and later > Settings catalog.
Add the Microsoft Edge Update settings: keep the Stable channel and enable automatic updates.
Assign to the Windows device group.
Note: LOW impact — keeps Edge current on the Stable channel.

requiredSettings.label: Policy type
requiredSettings.value: Devices > Configuration > Settings catalog profile (Microsoft Defender update settings)

requiredSettings.label: Security intelligence update interval
requiredSettings.value: Frequent checks (e.g. every few hours) so signatures stay current

requiredSettings.label: Update channels
requiredSettings.value: Platform / engine / security-intelligence channels per baseline (typically default/GA cadence)

steps: Intune admin center > Devices > Configuration > Create > Windows 10 and later > Settings catalog.
Add the Microsoft Defender signature/engine update settings: set the security-intelligence update interval and update channels.
Assign to the Windows device group.
Note: LOW impact — keeps Defender AV definitions/engine fresh; complements the Defender Antivirus protection policy (Phase 2).

requiredSettings.label: Feature
requiredSettings.value: Windows Update for Business reports — cloud reporting on update compliance/alerts

requiredSettings.label: Prerequisite
requiredSettings.value: Devices must send the required diagnostic data (Windows diagnostic data enabled via the telemetry config profile)

steps: Enable Windows Update for Business reports (Reports > Windows updates, or the WUFB reports workbook) and confirm the reporting configuration.
Ensure devices send required diagnostic data (see the Telemetry config profile in Phase 6) so they show up in the reports.
Review update compliance and the Alerts data type for devices failing updates.
Note: LOW impact / admin-only — reporting/visibility, no device-facing change.
